seoTitle: Eva Valley vs Fannie Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Eva Valley and Fannie Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Eva Valley vs Fannie Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Eva Valley and Fannie Bay.
heading: Eva Valley vs Fannie Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Eva Valley, Northern Territory and Fannie Bay, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Eva Valley has a lower overall climate risk score (25/100) compared to Fannie Bay (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Eva Valley or Fannie Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Eva Valley has a flood risk score of 20/100, while Fannie Bay scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Eva Valley scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Fannie Bay scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Eva Valley or Fannie Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Eva Valley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
